skill.name: emergency-card
skill.description: Creates portable emergency medical information cards from existing health records, including allergies, medications, conditions, implants, and emergency contacts.
skill.summary: Create emergency medical cards for travel, care visits, and urgent situations.
skill.author: huifer

security_audit.summary: Static command, weak-crypto, and obfuscation alerts were reviewed in context and are mostly false positives from Markdown code fences, multilingual text, sample JSON, and example shell commands. The confirmed risk is privacy exposure because the skill reads local health files and creates portable cards, QR payloads, and optional network-dependent sharing paths for sensitive medical information.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Medical Data Exported to Portable Files
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to read local health records and save emergency-card files and QR content. This is useful for the skill purpose, but it can expose allergies, medications, diagnoses, implants, and contacts if the generated files are shared or stored insecurely.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file access and saved outputs are directly documented. The privacy risk follows from the sensitive medical fields listed in the same workflow.

medium_findings.title: Optional Network-Dependent Sharing of Medical Data
medium_findings.description: The documentation mentions CDN dependencies, cloud backup, and a short-link QR option. These are not malicious, but they can move sensitive emergency-card data outside local storage if a user follows them without privacy review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network references are explicit, but they are presented as optional formats and examples rather than automatic exfiltration code.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Are Mostly Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many backticks as Ruby or shell execution, but the files are Markdown documentation. The only meaningful command examples are documented Python invocations and no executable script file is included in the scanned file set.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content appears inside Markdown fenced examples. No evidence found of hidden command execution outside documentation.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Obfuscation Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: Static high-severity alerts for weak cryptography and high entropy were reviewed. No cryptographic implementation, encoded payload, or obfuscated executable content was found in the cited Markdown locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are frontmatter or ordinary JavaScript object examples. The entropy signal is likely caused by multilingual medical text and Markdown formatting.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Health File Reads and Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow reads health data from local JSON files and writes generated emergency-card files. This should run only with user consent and in a trusted workspace.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file read and save operations are directly shown in the skill instructions. The pattern is legitimate for the use case but sensitive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network-Backed Card Distribution Option
dangerous_patterns.description: The QR format documentation includes a short-link option that depends on a server. Users should prefer offline QR content or encrypted storage for medical data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URL is a placeholder and not a live exfiltration endpoint. The risk is the documented pattern of server-backed access to medical data.

content.value_statement: Important medical details are hard to find during urgent care. This skill summarizes allergies, medications, conditions, implants, and contacts into portable emergency cards.
actual_capabilities: Extracts key details from existing profile, allergy, medication, radiation, surgery, and discharge-summary records.
Prioritizes severe allergies, active medications, urgent conditions, implants, and emergency contacts.
Defines outputs for HTML, JSON, plain text, PDF, and QR-code card formats.
Supports card variants for adults, children, older adults, and severe allergy cases.
Adds disclaimers and reminds users that the card does not replace medical diagnosis.
Provides examples for travel, school, elder care, healthy adults, and severe allergy scenarios.
limitations: It does not verify medical accuracy or replace a clinician review.
It depends on existing local health data being complete and current.
Generated cards may expose sensitive health data if shared, uploaded, or printed carelessly.
Referenced generator scripts are documented, but no script file is present in this skill package.

use_cases.title: Prepare Travel Documents
use_cases.description: Create a compact card with allergies, medicines, implants, and emergency contacts before a trip.

use_cases.title: Support Older Adults Living Alone
use_cases.description: Generate a large-print emergency card with current medicines, conditions, and family contacts.

use_cases.title: Share Child Health Details With School
use_cases.description: Prepare a school-focused card with allergies, asthma triggers, parent contacts, and basic instructions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Card
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a standard emergency card.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an emergency medical information card from my available health records. Include severe allergies, current medicines, conditions, implants, and emergency contacts.

prompt_templates.title: Make a Travel Card
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing for travel with printed and phone-ready formats.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a travel emergency medical card for next week. Prioritize allergies, medicines, implants, and contacts. Suggest text and QR formats.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Caregiver Card
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a large-print card for an older adult.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a large-print emergency card for an older adult who lives alone. Include medications, chronic conditions, emergency contacts, and a clear disclaimer.

prompt_templates.title: Review Severe Allergy Output
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review for high-risk allergy information.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a severe allergy emergency card and review it for missing source dates, unclear warnings, privacy risks, and any medical claims that need clinician confirmation.

output_examples.input: I need a card for travel next week.
output_examples.output: A travel-ready emergency card listing severe allergies first, followed by active medicines, implants, emergency contacts, and update reminders.

output_examples.input: Create a card for my father who lives alone.
output_examples.output: A large-print card with medicine count, chronic conditions, family contacts, and placement suggestions for home and wallet copies.

output_examples.input: My child needs a school medical information sheet.
output_examples.output: A school-focused card with allergies, asthma triggers, parent contacts, school clinic details, and clear safety notes.

best_practices: Review every generated card with the patient or caregiver before using it.
Regenerate the card after medication, allergy, diagnosis, or contact changes.
Use offline storage or encrypted sharing for cards that contain sensitive health data.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated content as medical advice or a diagnosis.
Do not upload QR cards or medical summaries to public links.
Do not include outdated medicines, unverified allergies, or unreachable contacts.

faq.question: Can this skill diagnose medical problems?
faq.answer: No. It summarizes recorded information and adds disclaimers. A licensed clinician must make diagnoses and treatment decisions.

faq.question: Which formats does it describe?
faq.answer: It describes HTML, JSON, text, PDF, and QR-code formats for different emergency and care settings.

faq.question: Does it need existing health records?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow expects existing profile, allergy, medication, procedure, and discharge-summary records.

faq.question: Is the generated card private?
faq.answer: Only if you store and share it privately. Printed cards, QR images, cloud backups, and short links can expose sensitive data.

faq.question: Can I use it for a child or older adult?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation includes child, older adult, standard, and severe allergy card variants.

faq.question: Should the card be updated often?
faq.answer: Yes. Update it when health information changes, before travel, and after medication or contact changes.
